title: Visual Encoders: Mapping Images to Tokens
concept: A visual encoder (like a ViT, callback to Unit 176) converts an image into a grid of visual tokens, representing them in the same embedding space as text.

exercises.prompt: To help a text-based LLM understand an image, what does a visual encoder convert the image into?
exercises.explain: A visual encoder takes an image and converts it into visual tokens so that a language model can process it alongside text.

cards.title: Teaching Language Models to See
cards.body: Large Language Models are masters of reading text, but they are completely blind to raw image files! How do we get a text-loving brain to understand a colorful photograph? We use a translator called a Visual Encoder.

cards.title: What is a Visual Encoder?
cards.body: A visual encoder is a neural network component (often a Vision Transformer or ViT) that takes a raw image, slices it into a grid of patches, and converts each patch into a "visual token." These tokens are mathematical vectors that live in the same embedding space as text tokens.

cards.title: The Comic Strip Split
cards.body: Imagine translating a large comic book page into a structured script. Instead of describing the entire page at once, you divide it into a grid of panels. You translate each panel into a short paragraph of text, aligning all panels into a clean sequence of written descriptions that a writer can easily read from top to bottom.

cards.title: Seeing in Squares
cards.body: When you upload a photo of a dog sitting in a park to a multimodal AI, the visual encoder breaks the photo into a 16x16 grid of small square patches. It translates the "grassy patch," the "furry patch," and the "sky patch" into vectors that the LLM processes just like the words "grass," "dog," and "sky."

cards.title: Myth: Models See the Whole Picture at Once
cards.body: Many people think visual encoders analyze the whole image as a single, giant file. In reality, they chop the image into a grid of independent patches first. This allows the model to pay attention to specific details in different parts of the image, just like reading words in a sentence!

cards.title: Pixels vs. Tokens
cards.body: Visual encoders act as the bridge between raw pixels and semantic tokens that language models can understand.

right.text: A sequence of vector embeddings representing image patches aligned in the text model's vocabulary space.

left.text: A grid of raw pixel color values (RGB numbers) that have no text-like meaning to an LLM.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary job of a Vision Transformer (ViT) serving as a visual encoder?

options.text: It turns text sentences into synthetic images.
It slices an image into patches and converts them into tokens.
It increases the resolution of blurry photos.
It deletes the image to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: A Vision Transformer (ViT) acts as the visual encoder, chopping the image into patches and mapping them into the same embedding space as text tokens.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Visual tokens are formatted to exist in the same mathematical space as text tokens so the LLM can understand both.
exercises.explain: Yes! By mapping visual tokens into the same embedding space as text tokens, the LLM can process both modalities together.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how images are prepared for the LLM.
exercises.sentence: A visual encoder turns an image into a sequence of visual [blank] that the AI can read.
exercises.explain: Visual encoders transform image patches into visual tokens, which are represented in the same embedding space as text.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is spoken by a real Visual Encoder?

options.text: "I am a visual encoder, and I generate text tokens from audio waveforms."
"I am a visual encoder, and I convert image patches into vector embeddings in the text space."
"I am a visual encoder, and I print physical photos from text prompts."

exercises.explain: The visual encoder generates visual tokens (embeddings), whereas the text tokenizer handles text. The LLM then processes both token types together.

exercises.prompt: How does a Visual Encoder help a Multimodal LLM process an image alongside text?

options.text: It processes the image into visual tokens that share the same embedding space as text tokens.
It translates the text prompt into pixels so the image can understand it.
It compresses the image into a single JPEG file for the model to download.
It converts text tokens into high-resolution PNG images.

exercises.explain: A visual encoder maps raw image pixels into a sequence of vector embeddings (visual tokens) that lie in the same mathematical space as text tokens, allowing the multimodal LLM to read both seamlessly.
